data.title: Colombia 12-month inflation rate at 5.16% in April, above forecasts
data.description:
Colombia's consumer price index rose 0.66% in April, the country's DANE statistics agency said on Thursday, bringing the South American economy's 12-month inflation rate to 5.16%.

The data was higher than expected by analysts in a Reuters poll, who had on average predicted a 0.48% rise in monthly consumer prices and annual inflation of 4.97%.

Analysts lifted their average inflation forecast for the end of this year to 4.55% from a prior forecast of 4.42%. If the forecast proves accurate, Colombia would miss its 3% inflation target for the fifth year running.

April's price hikes were led by increases in food and housing.

The information and communication sector was the only one that registered a monthly decrease on its costs.

Colombia's central bank cut the benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 9.25% last month, surprising the market, amid slightly reduced inflation predictions, fiscal difficulties and a cloudy international economic outlook.

data.title: Alaska Air Group announces leadership promotions in key enterprise oversight roles
data.description:
Alaska Air Group announces leadership promotions in key enterprise oversight roles

PR Newswire

SEATTLE, May 8, 2025

SEATTLE, May 8, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The Alaska Air Group board of directors has placed two veteran executives in key oversight roles to enhance safety and internal audit functions at Alaska Airlines, Hawaiian Airlines, Horizon Air and McGee Air Services. These appointments support safety as the Company's top priority and Alaska and Hawaiian's continued international growth.

The reorganized leadership structure involves:

  • Brooke Vatheuer, who has been promoted to senior vice president of safety and audit programs for Alaska Air Group and Alaska Airlines;
  • Max Tidwell, who remains vice president of safety and security for Alaska Air Group and Alaska Airlines; and
  • Allie Wittenberger, who has been elected vice president of audit programs for Alaska Airlines

"This is a transformational time for Alaska Air Group, and I'm grateful to our team who is leveling up across the organization as we emerge a stronger airline with more global presence," said Alaska CEO Ben Minicucci. "Brooke and Allie are trusted leaders who reflect our Alaska values and continue to lend valuable safety and audit oversight as we grow and introduce ourselves to even more of the world."

Brooke Vatheuer — AAG Senior Vice President of Safety and Audit Programs

As the senior vice president of safety and audit programs for Alaska Air Group, Brooke Vatheuer will oversee both the safety and aviation security programs across Alaska Airlines, Hawaiian Airlines, Horizon Air and McGee Air Services, as well as the internal audit function that examines, evaluates and reports on Alaska Air Group's business and operational processes. The safety and audit teams will remain directly accountable to committees of the Board of Directors and Vatheuer will lead both teams' strategies to ensure the strongest focus on safety and accountability.

Max Tidwell will continue his role as vice president of safety and security, where he oversees all safety functions at Air Group companies and drives development of long-term safety and security strategies.

Vatheuer joined Alaska Airlines in 2007 as an internal auditor and quickly took on leadership roles including Horizon Air's vice president of finance and planning and senior vice president of operations and planning. In 2020, Vatheuer transitioned to vice president of strategic performance for Alaska Airlines, overseeing performance for all operational groups in the daily Seattle operation and most recently served as the vice president of audit programs. She has a master's degree in professional accounting and bachelor's degree in business administration from the University of Washington.

Allie Wittenberger — Alaska Airlines Vice President of Audit Programs

Allie Wittenberger, in her new role as vice president of audit programs, will set the strategic vision and lead enterprise-level audit and assurance programs. Wittenberger will lead a team whose mission is to continuously improve Alaska's systems of financial, safety, operational, organizational and technological controls.

Wittenberger joined Alaska in 2010 as a senior internal auditor. She has held numerous leadership positions in the audit program, financial planning and analysis and ethics and compliance. Wittenberger most recently served as Alaska Air Group's managing director of corporate affairs and compliance where she supported Board of Director functions, corporate governance and compliance programs. She received a bachelor's degree in accounting and finance from Washington State University and is a certified public accountant.

About Alaska Air Group

Alaska Airlines, Hawaiian Airlines and Horizon Air are subsidiaries of Alaska Air Group, with McGee Air Services a subsidiary of Alaska Airlines. With hubs in Seattle, Honolulu, Portland, Anchorage, Los Angeles, San Diego and San Francisco, we deliver remarkable care as we fly our guests to more than 140 destinations throughout North America, Latin America, Asia and the Pacific. Alaska is a member of the oneworld Alliance with Hawaiian scheduled to join in 2026. With oneworld and our additional global partners, guests can earn and redeem miles for travel to over 1,000 worldwide destinations. Guests can book travel at alaskaair.com and hawaiianairlines.com. Learn more about what's happening at Alaska and Hawaiian. Alaska Air Group is traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) as "ALK."

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/alaska-air-group-announces-leadership-promotions-in-key-enterprise-oversight-roles-302450708.html

SOURCE Alaska Air Group

data.title: GENERAL AMERICAN INVESTORS CO President & CEO Acquires Preferred Stock
data.description:
Reporter NamePriest Jeffrey W
RelationshipPresident & CEO
TypePurchase
Amount$28,240
SEC FilingForm 4

Jeffrey W Priest, President & CEO of GENERAL AMERICAN INVESTORS CO, purchased 1,141 shares of the company's 5.95% Preferred Stock on May 8, 2025, at a price of $24.7506 per share, totaling $28,240. Following this transaction, Priest directly owns 18,268 shares of 5.95% Preferred Stock and 94,584 shares of Common Stock. Indirectly, Priest owns 157,515 shares of Common Stock and 27,241 shares of 5.95% Preferred Stock through various means including by parent, power of attorney, and thrift plan trust.

SEC Filing: GENERAL AMERICAN INVESTORS CO INC [ GAM ] - Form 4 - May. 08, 2025

data.title: Property For Industry Declares Quarterly Dividend, Raises Guidance for Fiscal Year 2025
data.description:
Property For Industry declared a fiscal third quarter dividend of NZ$0.021 per share, according to a Friday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

The dividend is payable on May 27 to shareholders of record as of May 16, the filing showed.

The company raised its cash dividend guidance to NZ$0.086 from its previous guidance of NZ$0.085, resulting in a fiscal fourth quarter and final dividend of NZ$0.025, the filing added.

For fiscal year 2026, Property for Industry expects to declare dividends from NZ$0.088 to NZ$0.089.

data.title: Alcoa Appoints Non-Executive Chairman
data.description:
Alcoa appointed Thomas Gorman as non-executive chairman, effective Thursday, according to a same-day filing with the Australian bourse.

Gorman will succeed outgoing Non-Executive Chairman Steven Williams.

data.title: ANZ Target Price Cut 1.8% to A$27.50/Share by Macquarie>ANZ.AU
S. Korea Posts Current Account Surplus for 11th Month
data.description:
South Korea recorded a current account surplus of $9.14 billion in March 2025, marking the eleventh consecutive month in positive territory, driven by strong exports.

The surplus rose from $7.18 billion in February and $6.99 billion in March 2024.

The goods account posted a surplus of $8.49 billion, as exports grew 2.2% year-on-year to $59.31 billion, while imports rose 2.3% to $50.82 billion.

The services account registered a $2.21 billion deficit, primarily due to shortfalls in the travel and other business services categories.

Meanwhile, the primary income account showed a $3.23 billion surplus, reflecting increased income from equity investments.

The secondary income account recorded a $0.37 billion deficit.

data.title: Orica Target Price Raised 2.7% to A$21.48/Share by Macquarie>ORI.AU
Generation Development Group's Alliance Could Generate Consistent Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Generation Development Group's agreement with BlackRock to co-design and distribute a new product aimed at providing tailored solutions for Australian retirees gets a tick from Jefferies. It views the alliance as an incremental step that could lead to consistent growth for GDG over many years. "At this early stage, product development will be the priority, followed by adviser education," analyst Simon Fitzgerald says. "These elements are essential to building a successful product, even if the time frame to meaningful earnings growth is difficult to judge." Jefferies highlights the example of GDG's Lifetime annuity. It launched in 2022 and is yet to reach scale. (david.winning@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 7 PM ET
data.description:
U.S. and U.K. Unveil Framework for Trade Deal

President Trump lauded the agreement as the first with a major trading partner since he imposed blanket tariffs on countries worldwide.

EU Targets American Aircraft, Car Parts for Possible Tariffs if Talks Fail

The bloc said it could target more than $100 billion worth of American products with tariffs if negotiations with the U.S. break down.

Stocks Gain After Trump Touts U.K. Deal, High Hopes on China

The Dow industrials climb 0.6%; Nasdaq adds 1.1%.

New York Fed Survey Finds Consumers Ambivalent on Inflation Outlook

Consumers sent mixed signals about their inflation expectations in the New York Fed's April survey. But the poll showed no serious rise in long-term inflation expectations.

Bank of England Cuts Rates After Fed Stands Pat

The Bank of England cut rates, highlighting a growing gap in how global central banks and the Federal Reserve are approaching Trump's tariffs.

Jobless Claims Fell Last Week

The Labor Department said that 228,000 filed new jobless claims in the week through May 3, down from 241,000 a week earlier. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal had expected to see 230,000 new filings.

Canada's Financial Stability Faces Stiff Test From U.S. Tariffs, Bank of Canada Says

Annual central bank report says there is a risk permanent tariffs could cause high unemployment and business insolvencies.

Car Insurance Rates Were Ready to Drop. Then Tariffs Came Along.

Insurers have slowed requests to regulators for rate decreases.

Fentanyl Crisis Provided Opening for U.S.-China Trade Talks

A planned meeting in Switzerland will be the first direct talks over trade during Trump's second term.

U.S. to Overhaul Curbs on AI Chip Exports After Industry Backlash

A rule limiting many countries' access to American technology was set to take effect May 15.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 7 PM ET
data.description:
Lyft Swings to Profit Amid Increase in Ridership

San Francisco-based company said rides grew 16% year over year, to 218.4 million, ahead of analyst views for 215.1 million.

McKesson to Spin Off Medical-Surgical Company

The pharmaceutical distributor said the new company would be a differentiated medical surgical supply and solutions business.

Paramount Notches Streaming Growth, but Revenue Drops

The company saw gains in its streaming business, but its overall revenue declined compared with the same period last year when it televised the Super Bowl.

News Corp Revenue Boosted by Gains at Dow Jones, HarperCollins

Digital and print Wall Street Journal subscriptions totaled 4.34 million for the March quarter, the company says.

Coinbase Reports Drop in Profit

Earlier in the day Coinbase announced a $2.9 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire Deribit, a bitcoin options exchange.

TKO Group Raises Full-Year Outlook on Recent Acquisitions, Swings to Profit

The new forecasts came as TKO Group swung to a profit and posted higher-than-expected revenue in the recent quarter.

Air Canada Loss Widens, Full-Year Outlook Lowered on Economic Uncertainty

Chief Executive Michael Rousseau said he expects market conditions to be unsteady moving forward as the economic outlook appears uncertain

Funko Pulls Outlook as Loss Widens, Sales Fall

The company said it accelerated plans to diversify its supply chain, especially with regard to imports from China.

Trump's Tariffs Aim to Boost Steelmakers. One of the Biggest Is Shrinking Instead.

Cleveland-Cliffs closes plants and slows expansion but is still counting on tariffs to increase demand from automakers.

Celsius's Mashinsky, Former Crypto High-Roller, Gets 12-Year Prison Sentence

Federal prosecutors had sought a sentence of at least 20 years for Alex Mashinsky, who attracted customers-many of them ordinary investors-by urging them to "unbank" themselves and park their savings at Celsius.

data.title: Light & Wonder Target Price Cut 5.6% to A$187.00/Share by Macquarie>LNW.AU
Colombia’s Inflation Rises to 5.16% in April
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Colombia inched up to 5.16% in April 2025, from 5.09% in March, coming in above market expectations of 5%.

The uptick was driven by faster price increases in key categories such as transportation (+5.29% vs. +4.88% in March), restaurants and hotels (+7.71% vs. +7.34%), health (+5.31% vs. +5.30%), and education (+7.38% vs. +7.27%).

In contrast, inflation eased slightly for food (+4.61% vs. +4.67%) and housing (+6.09% vs. +6.29%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.66%, accelerating from March’s 0.52% increase and slightly above the market forecast of 0.5%.

data.title: An Investigation Has Commenced on Behalf of Cable One, Inc. Shareholders. Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Discuss your CABO Losses.
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 8, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Cable One, Inc. ("Cable One, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On May 2, 2025, Cable One missed earnings expectations by nearly $8 and suspended its dividend. Analysts at Keybanc called the results "extremely dissapointing" and questioned the credibility of Cable One's management. Raymond James also criticized Cable One's management, claiming that "The results for Q1 were worse than expected, yet management was adamant they will see growth in both broadband top line revenue and subscribers for the year." Cable One's announcement contrasts with their prior messaging, which emphasized confidence in growth across multiple of the company's segments.

Following this news, shares of Cable One, Inc. fell over 40% in intraday trading on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/cable-one-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147643&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: QBE Insurance Group Posts Higher Gross Written Premium in Q1, Reaffirms 2025 Outlook
data.description:
QBE Insurance Group reported Friday that its gross written premium in the first quarter rose to $8.32 billion, up 8% on a constant currency basis from $7.77 billion a year earlier.

Total investment funds under management were $31.6 billion, up from $30.6 billion a year earlier.

In the four months to April, the company reported a net cost of catastrophe claims of about $420 million, driven by the LA wildfires, flooding in Queensland, cyclone Alfred, and a series of storms in North America.

The insurance group reaffirmed its 2025 gross written premium growth in the mid-single digits on a constant currency basis, a combined operating ratio of about 93%.

data.title: Singapore's bank OCBC first-quarter net profit drops 5%, but beats forecast
data.description:
Singapore's second-largest bank Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp (OCBC) reported on Friday a 5% decline in net profit in the first quarter, the first drop since the first quarter of 2022, mainly on lower net interest income.

OCBC, which is also Southeast Asia's second-largest lender, said January-March net profit fell to S$1.88 billion ($1.45 billion) from a record S$1.98 billion a year earlier.

This, however, beat the mean estimate of S$1.87 billion from two analysts polled by LSEG.

($1 = 1.3002 Singapore dollars)

data.title: Peru’s Cuts Rate to 4.5%
data.description:
The central bank of Peru lowered its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 4.5% during its May 2025 meeting, ending a three-month pause in rate adjustments.

The move brings the policy rate closer to its estimated neutral level, with future changes dependent on evolving inflation dynamics and economic data.

In April, headline inflation rose 0.32% month-over-month, while core inflation—excluding food and energy—came in at 0.14%.

Annual inflation increased to 1.7%, up from 1.3% in March, largely driven by food prices, although core inflation remained stable at 1.9%, consistent with the bank’s target range.

Inflation expectations over the next 12 months held steady at 2.3%, also within the target band.

The Central Reserve Bank of Peru (BCRP) acknowledged a slight weakening in both current and expected economic activity indicators, but noted they remained generally optimistic, signaling that the economy is likely operating near its potential.

data.title: Ready Capital Corp reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Ready Capital Corp reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 9 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 29 cents. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 12 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 9 cents to 14 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 70.1% to $14.50 million from a year ago; analysts expected $53.94 million.

  • Ready Capital Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 46 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $77.51 million.

  • Ready Capital Corp shares had fallen by 13.4% this quarter and lost 35.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 36.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the specialized reits peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ready Capital Corp is $5.50

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data May 8 at 11:09 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated.
